meta.title: Patryk Jabłoński | DevOps, Cloud & Systems Engineer
meta.description: Portfolio of Patryk Jabłoński, a DevOps, Cloud & Systems Engineer specializing in Microsoft Azure, Terraform, CI/CD, automation, and production environments.

hero.name: Patryk Jabłoński
hero.headline: DevOps & Systems Engineer focused on Microsoft Azure.
hero.description: I automate infrastructure and deployments with Terraform, CI/CD, PowerShell and Bash. I combine 7+ years in IT with hands-on ownership of Windows and Linux production environments.

about.title: About me
about.subtitle: Infrastructure, automation and ownership of production environments.
about.paragraph1: I work as a Systems and DevOps Engineer, specializing in Azure. I specialize in maintaining business-critical environments, designing cloud infrastructure, automating deployments, and improving system security and observability.
about.paragraph2: My day-to-day work combines Azure Cloud, Terraform, CI/CD, Docker, PowerShell, Bash, Windows and Linux operating systems as well as databases. I care not only about making a solution work, but also about making it repeatable, documented, secure, and easy to evolve.
about.paragraph3: Alongside engineering, I teach technology-related courses and conduct research focused on artificial intelligence, automation, and digital transformation.

projects.title: Projects

featured.title: Azure Terraform Docker Guestbook (Under development)
featured.tagline: A complete CI/CD process for a containerized .NET application deployed to Microsoft Azure.
featured.description: This project demonstrates the complete process of designing, provisioning, securing, and deploying a cloud application. Infrastructure is created with Terraform, the application runs in Docker, and GitHub Actions handles infrastructure planning and deployment, testing, security scanning, image build, and release to Azure Container Apps.

networkLab.title: Azure Network Infrastructure Lab (Under development)
networkLab.tagline: A teaching project prepared for Network Infrastructure Management students.
networkLab.description: An Infrastructure as Code lab created as a teaching example for Network Infrastructure Management students. The project deploys an Azure Resource Group, a Virtual Network, three network segments, Network Security Groups, Windows Server virtual machines, and an Ubuntu virtual machine prepared for security monitoring with Wazuh. It demonstrates network segmentation, restricted backend access, and repeatable environment provisioning with Terraform.
